title: Where the Canopy Speaks in Currents

features.name: root-circuit-first
features.desc-on-touch: a chip embedded in root-matrix; the canopy has learned to think through soil, and thought travels as current.

features.name: water-trace-singing
features.desc-on-touch: signal paths drawn by water that flows through leaves as though through circuits; the canopy has learned another way to speak.

features.name: leaf-logic-gathering
features.desc-on-touch: where chlorophyll becomes computation; the canopy comparing light and dark, building arguments in photosynthesis.

features.name: node-where-currents-converge
features.desc-on-touch: all the water paths meet here; all the light paths; the canopy has learned to be a network, not a forest.

features.name: silence-trace-deepening
features.desc-on-touch: where the canopy learns that not all speech is sound; the deepest grammar flows where nothing moves but meaning.

inscription: the canopy has learned a second language and forgot it speaks in words

file.name: Spectrogram_Canopy_Frequency_Analysis_Undated.png
file.note: the archive labeled this as audio; the sound is water and leaves, but neither one is making it

exits.label: back to where water first learned to breathe
deeper where the circuit descends into root
toward the canopy's edge where signals fade to weather
up through leaf-layers that have learned to transmit
